body.title: [sprint] Add !CategoryTemplate.json to each memory-type root (self-documenting field schema)
body.intention: I intend that each devlab/runtime/memory/<category>/ root carries a !CategoryTemplate.json naming every field and its purpose, so the emission shape is self-documenting at the point of use.
body.description:
Akien (2026-06-25): it'd be nice to have each memory type carry, in its root, a !CategoryTemplate.json documenting all the fields and what each is used for. The '!' prefix sorts it to the top of the directory listing so it's the first thing a reader (human or CC) sees when opening a category dir. One template per category under devlab/runtime/memory/ (decisions, tickets, slates, sessions, rules, proofs, design_patterns, notes, projects, architecture, builder_feedback, judge, librarian, boot, artifacts). Each template documents the shared envelope fields (id, emitter, namespace, category, kind, emitted_at, links) plus the category-specific body fields, each with a one-line 'what it's for'. Source of truth is the emission shape produced by memory_emit.py + devlab/runtime/memory/SPEC.md; the templates are a derived, human-facing reference (ideally generated from the same field definitions so they can't drift).

**Affected files:** new devlab/runtime/memory/<category>/!CategoryTemplate.json per category; possibly a small generator in devlab/claudecode/ that emits them from memory_emit.py field defs + SPEC.md; no change to memory_emit.py write path.
**Design rules:** flat-file (no DB); canonical memory home is devlab/runtime/memory/ (one home, no exceptions); writes normally go through memory_emit.py — these templates are static reference docs, not emissions, so they are the documented exception (like SPEC.md).
**Scope boundary:** IN — one !CategoryTemplate.json per existing category root + the field documentation; optionally a generator so they regenerate rather than drift. OUT — changing the emission schema itself, validation/enforcement that emissions match the template, retrofitting historical files.
**Test plan:** assert every category dir under devlab/runtime/memory/ that memory_emit.py can write to has a !CategoryTemplate.json; assert each template lists the shared envelope fields. If a generator is built, assert it reproduces the committed templates idempotently (red→green a hollow build couldn't pass).
**Completion criteria:** every active memory category root contains a !CategoryTemplate.json documenting its fields; a test proves presence + envelope-field coverage for >=1 category (ideally all).

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
